articles.title: Karnataka braces for severe heatwave: red alert across multiple districts - NewsFirst Prime
articles.full_text:
Karnataka faces severe heatwave with red alerts for districts like Raichur and Kalaburagi, temps over 40°C. Health advisories urge hydration and avoiding midday sun
Karnataka is gripped by an intense heatwave, with temperatures soaring above 40°C in several districts, triggering red alerts from the India Meteorological Department (IMD) and Karnataka State Natural Disaster Monitoring Centre (KSNDMC). The scorching conditions, persisting through mid-April, pose serious health risks, especially in northern interiors.
Northern districts including Raichur, Kalaburagi, Yadgir, Ballari, and Vijayapura face the highest danger, with forecasts predicting peaks of 41-45°C or more. Coastal regions like Mangaluru experience oppressive humidity, pushing "feels-like" temperatures to 50°C, while Bengaluru records highs around 37°C. Authorities warn of prolonged discomfort until early May, urging immediate precautions.
Health officials advise residents to avoid outdoor activities between 11 AM and 4 PM, stay hydrated with water and electrolyte drinks, and wear light cotton clothing. Employers must ensure shaded rest areas and ample water for outdoor workers. Symptoms like dizziness, nausea, headaches, and rapid heartbeat signal heat stress—seek cool areas and medical help promptly.

The heatwave exacerbates urban vulnerabilities, straining power grids and water supplies amid rising demand. Schools in affected areas may shift timings, and farmers face crop stress in unirrigated fields. Last year's patterns showed similar extremes, with heat-related illnesses surging 30%.
KSNDMC Director G.S. Srinivas Reddy emphasized community vigilance: "Early action saves lives—prioritize vulnerable groups like the elderly and labourers." Public awareness campaigns via radio, SMS, and apps reinforce safety measures.
As summer intensifies, Karnataka's residents adapt to this annual trial, balancing endurance with proactive defence against nature's fury.
